description: Customer support ticket management - view tickets, reply, send direct messages, update status

endpoints.name: Get All Complaints/Tickets

frontendUsage.frontendDescription: Displays ticket list with client name, email, avatar, status badge, date, and message preview. Deduplicates IDs automatically.

endpoints.frontendDisplay: Ticket list with client avatar, name, email, status badge (PENDING=yellow, REPLIED=green, RESOLVED=gray), date, and last message preview. Click opens chat thread.

endpoints.name: Reply To Complaint
endpoints.description: Send an admin reply to a support ticket

frontendUsage.frontendDescription: Called when admin types and sends a reply in the ticket chat thread. Optionally includes a file attachment.
frontendUsage.mockStatus: USE_MOCK=true - appends message to ticket's messages array

text.example: We apologize for the inconvenience. Your refund has been processed.

endpoints.frontendDisplay: New admin message appears in chat thread with sender 'admin', timestamp, and optional file attachment link. Ticket status changes to 'REPLIED'.

endpoints.name: Send Direct Message To User
endpoints.description: Send an unsolicited message to a user (creates ticket if none exists)

frontendUsage.usedBy: complaintService.js (Admin Support Page, User Management Page)
frontendUsage.frontendDescription: Called when admin sends a direct message to a customer from the Users page. Creates new ticket or replies to existing conversation.
frontendUsage.mockStatus: USE_MOCK=true - finds or creates ticket by customer email

endpoints.frontendDisplay: Success toast, if ticket existed message appears in existing thread, if new ticket is created it shows in the ticket list

endpoints.name: Update Complaint Status
endpoints.description: Update the status of a support ticket

frontendUsage.frontendDescription: Called when admin changes ticket status (e.g., PENDING -> REPLIED -> RESOLVED)

endpoints.frontendDisplay: Status badge color updates on the ticket in the list view

endpoints.name: Delete Complaint

frontendUsage.frontendDescription: Called when admin confirms ticket deletion via confirmation dialog

endpoints.frontendDisplay: Confirmation dialog, success toast, ticket removed from list
